findings.title: Global restore/uninstall skill pruning can walk above CODEX_HOME
findings.rationale: After a successful restore or uninstall, both commands call prune_empty_skill_dirs with repo_root as the stopping boundary. The helper derives parent directories directly from manifest skill_file paths and prune_empty_dir_chain only stops when the current directory equals repo_root. For global setup manifests, target resolution already treats scope=user paths as living under CODEX_HOME/user_root, which can be outside the project repo. In a global rollback where setup created config.toml, hooks.json, and copied skills under an otherwise empty CODEX_HOME, the transaction deletes those files and the pruning loop can remove CODEX_HOME and continue into its empty parent because repo_root is unrelated. Existing qualification tests verify config/hooks restoration and that selected files disappear, but they do not assert that CODEX_HOME or its parent remain intact for the all-created global setup case.
findings.suggested_next_action: Make skill directory pruning scope-aware: resolve each skill_file entry to its target path and stop at repo_root for project scope and user_root/CODEX_HOME for user scope. Add restore and uninstall regression tests where global setup creates every managed path under a CODEX_HOME outside repo_root, then verify rollback does not prune above CODEX_HOME.
